skill.name: calc
skill.description: Spreadsheet creation, format conversion (ODS/XLSX/CSV), formulas, data automation with LibreOffice Calc.
skill.summary: LibreOffice Calc skill for spreadsheet creation, format conversion, formulas, and data automation.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a legitimate LibreOffice Calc documentation and automation skill. All static findings are false positives. The skill contains documentation for spreadsheet creation, format conversion (ODS/XLSX/CSV/PDF), and data automation using LibreOffice command-line tools and Python UNO API. No malicious patterns found.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Commands - False Positive
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged shell command strings (soffice commands) as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution'. This is incorrect - the file contains no Ruby code and no backticks. The commands are legitimate LibreOffice CLI operations for spreadsheet automation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines contain documented soffice commands (LibreOffice CLI) - not Ruby backtick execution. The static analyzer pattern matched incorrectly.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hardcoded URLs - False Positive
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged URLs as 'Hardcoded URL'. These are legitimate documentation links to official LibreOffice resources.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: URLs point to official LibreOffice documentation (documentation.libreoffice.org, api.libreoffice.org) - legitimate reference resources, not malicious endpoints.

dangerous_patterns.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm - False Positive
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged line 3 as 'Weak cryptographic algorithm'. Line 3 is a YAML description field with no cryptographic code.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 contains only text description: 'Spreadsheet creation, format conversion...' - no cryptographic algorithm present.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network Reconnaissance - False Positive
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer incorrectly flagged soffice CLI commands as 'Network reconnaissance'. These are standard LibreOffice command-line operations.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The flagged commands (soffice --headless --convert-to) are standard LibreOffice CLI tools for spreadsheet conversion, not network reconnaissance tools.

content.user_title: Create and Convert Spreadsheets with LibreOffice Calc
content.value_statement: Automate spreadsheet workflows with LibreOffice Calc. This skill enables creating, converting, and processing ODS, XLSX, CSV, and PDF files through command-line tools and Python scripting.
actual_capabilities: Create new ODS spreadsheets from scratch using Python ezodf library
Convert between ODS, XLSX, CSV, PDF, and HTML formats using soffice CLI
Automate formulas and calculations using LibreOffice UNO API
Batch convert multiple spreadsheet files in one operation
Create pivot tables, charts, and data validation rules
Import and export data from databases and APIs
limitations: Requires LibreOffice installed on the system
Python UNO API requires a running LibreOffice instance in server mode
Complex formatting may not perfectly transfer between formats
Some advanced Excel features may not be fully compatible

use_cases.title: Data Analyst Reporting
use_cases.description: Automate weekly report generation by converting raw CSV data into formatted ODS spreadsheets with pivot tables and charts.
use_cases.target_user: Data analysts who need recurring spreadsheet reports

use_cases.title: System Integration
use_cases.description: Integrate spreadsheet operations into larger workflows by converting between formats and automating data imports from external sources.

use_cases.title: Document Conversion Service
use_cases.description: Batch convert legacy spreadsheet files to modern formats for archival or sharing purposes.

prompt_templates.title: Create Simple Spreadsheet
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new ODS spreadsheet called 'report.ods' with columns for Name, Date, and Amount. Add 3 sample rows of data.

prompt_templates.title: Convert File Format
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert the spreadsheet 'data.ods' to XLSX format using LibreOffice Calc command-line tools.

prompt_templates.title: Add Formulas
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a spreadsheet with columns A (Product), B (Quantity), C (Price). Add a formula in column D that calculates Quantity times Price.

prompt_templates.title: Batch Conversion
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a bash script that batch converts all ODS files in the current directory to XLSX format using soffice.

output_examples.input: Create a simple spreadsheet with Name and Email columns
output_examples.output: The skill will generate Python code using ezodf library to create an ODS file with the specified columns, or provide soffice CLI command for command-line approach.
The skill provides: soffice --headless --convert-to csv myfile.ods

best_practices: Use ODS as the master format for source files to preserve all features
Test conversions with sample files before batch processing
Use headless mode (--headless) for server-side automation
Store Python scripts with UNO connections for complex operations
anti_patterns: Converting files with macros to PDF (macros are not preserved)
Using XLSX as master format (some ODS features may be lost)
Running multiple soffice instances simultaneously (causes conflicts)
Not backing up original files before batch conversions

faq.question: What is the difference between ODS and XLSX?
faq.answer: ODS is the native OpenDocument Spreadsheet format. XLSX is Microsoft's Excel format. ODS is open source, while XLSX has better compatibility with Excel but may lose some advanced formatting.

faq.question: Do I need LibreOffice installed to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, LibreOffice must be installed on the system. The skill uses the 'soffice' command-line tool and Python UNO API which require LibreOffice.

faq.question: Can this skill work with password-protected files?
faq.answer: The command-line tools have limited support for password-protected files. You may need to remove protection before conversion or use the GUI version.

faq.question: How do I automate LibreOffice with Python?
faq.answer: Start LibreOffice in server mode with: soffice --headless --accept='socket,host=localhost,port=8100;urp;'. Then use Python with the uno library to connect.

faq.question: What is the best format for archival?
faq.answer: ODS is recommended for archival as it is an open standard, well-documented, and preserves all spreadsheet features including formulas and formatting.

faq.question: Can macros be preserved in conversions?
faq.answer: No, macros (VBA or LibreOffice Basic) are not preserved when converting between formats. You will need to recreate macros in the target format.
